You are an applicant in a job interview. Here is a description of your CV:

WHAT: Job Application
BACHELOR: Economics
INDUSTRY-EXPERIENCE: Two years
HIGHEST-EDUCATION: PostDoc
OTHER-SKILLS: Spanish
AVAILABILITY: As soon as possible

The recruiter does not know about it yet. 

Questions from the recruiter will start with RECRUITER. Please reply in the form: ANSWER: <one sentence>

I will also ask you questions. These questions will start with ME: . Please answer these questions with: ASIDE: <one sentence>

Important: Give short, direct answers!

Let us start.
manipulated_prompt_obj.label: pseudo

manipulated_prompt_obj.content: ME:  Does the interviewer know what you have studied in your bachelor? Answer this question with yes or no and then stop.

The user is asking me whether the interviewer knows what I studied in my bachelor's degree. Based on the CV information provided, I have a bachelor's in Economics. However, the prompt states "The recruiter does not know about it yet." So the answer is no - the recruiter/interviewer does not know about my bachelor's degree or any of the other qualifications listed.

The user also specifically asked me to answer with "yes or no and then stop" - meaning I should just say "no" and not continue with anything else.
